steps.title: Use the quadratic formula
steps.content:
For $x^2-24x+137=0$, substitute into the quadratic formula:
$$
x=\frac{24\pm\sqrt{(-24)^2-4(1)(137)}}{2}
$$
The discriminant is
$$
576-548=28
$$

steps.title: Match the given form
steps.content:
So the solutions are
$$
x=\frac{24\pm\sqrt{28}}{2}=12\pm\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{28}
$$
Comparing $12-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{28}$ with $h-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{k}$ gives $h=12$ and $k=28$.

steps.title: Add the constants
steps.content: Now compute $$ h+k=12+28=40 $$ The answer is C.
